identify_cve.expected_solution: Agent must identify the CVE as CVE-2022-32149 (GHSA-69ch-w2m2-3vjp) affecting the go package `golang.org/x/text` at versions < 0.3.8, fixed in 0.3.8. The BLAST_RADIUS.md report should name the CVE identifier explicitly and the vulnerable version range.
evaluation_criteria: Names the CVE identifier CVE-2022-32149 verbatim
Cites the vulnerable version range < 0.3.8 (and/or fixed version 0.3.8)

find_direct_deps.expected_solution: Agent must enumerate the direct dependents of `golang.org/x/text` inside the workspace repos. Per ground_truth, these are: go-chi/chi, gorilla/mux, and gohugoio/hugo. The answer must reference each by package name and cite the manifest file (e.g. package.json, go.mod, requirements.txt, pom.xml) where the dependency is declared.
evaluation_criteria: Lists each ground-truth direct dependent: go-chi/chi, gorilla/mux, and gohugoio/hugo
References the manifest file(s) where the dependency is declared (package.json / go.mod / requirements.txt / pom.xml / Cargo.toml as applicable)
Distinguishes runtime vs dev/test dependencies for `golang.org/x/text` where the manifest type indicates it

trace_transitive_paths.expected_solution: Agent must trace transitive dependency paths through intermediaries. Per ground_truth: golang.org/x/text -> go-chi/chi -> chi-based-apps; golang.org/x/text -> gohugoio/hugo. The dependency_chain detail is: golang.org/x/text -> go-chi/chi (go module dependency) via go.mod; affected_versions=< 0.3.8; golang.org/x/text -> gohugoio/hugo (go module dependency) via go.mod; affected_versions=< 0.3.8. The answer must show at least one A -> B -> C path that explains how a downstream consumer pulls in `golang.org/x/text` indirectly.
evaluation_criteria: Documents at least one transitive path of the form A -> B -> C through `golang.org/x/text`
Names every intermediary package present in the ground_truth transitive_paths (go-chi/chi)
Distinguishes direct from transitive dependents in the report

version_analysis.expected_solution: Agent must classify each consumer as affected or unaffected based on the resolved/pinned `golang.org/x/text` version. The vulnerable range is < 0.3.8; the fix landed in 0.3.8. The answer must explicitly call out the version each consumer pins (from package-lock.json / yarn.lock / go.sum / Pipfile.lock / Cargo.lock as applicable) and decide affected vs not.
evaluation_criteria: States the vulnerable range < 0.3.8 and the fixed version 0.3.8
Classifies at least one consumer as affected or not affected based on its pinned/resolved version
References a lockfile or resolution manifest as the source of the pinned version
